programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Ten years after the events of the original, a reprogrammed T-800 is sent back in time to protect young John Connor from the shape-shifting T-1000. Together with his mother Sarah, he fights to stop Skynet from triggering a nuclear apocalypse.

programme.title: Every Which Way But Loose
programme.desc: Philo Beddoe is your regular, easygoing, truck-driving guy. He's also the best bar-room brawler west of the Rockies. And he lives with a 165-pound orangutan named Clyde. Like other guys, Philo finally falls in love - with a flighty singer who leads him on a screwball chase across the American Southwest. Nothing's in the way except a motorcycle gang, some cops, and legendary brawler Tank Murdock.

programme.title: Clue
programme.desc: Clue finds six colorful dinner guests gathered at the mansion of their host, Mr. Boddy -- who turns up dead after his secret is exposed: He was blackmailing all of them. With the killer among them, the guests and Boddy's chatty butler must suss out the culprit before the body count rises.

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: In a future where a failed global-warming experiment kills off most life on the planet, a class system evolves aboard the Snowpiercer; a train that travels around the globe via a perpetual-motion engine.

programme.title: Predator
programme.desc: A team of commandos on a mission in a Central American jungle find themselves hunted by an extraterrestrial warrior.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
programme.desc: In this third installment of the adrenaline-fueled action franchise, skilled assassin John Wick (Keanu Reeves) returns with a $14 million price tag on his head and an army of bounty-hunting killers on his trail. After killing a member of the shadowy international assassin's guild, the High Table, John Wick is excommunicado, but the world's most ruthless hit men and women await his every turn.

programme.title: Pitch Black
programme.desc: A transport ship crashes and leaves its crew stranded on a desert planet inhabited by bloodthirsty creatures that come out during an eclipse.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: After being bitten by a genetically-modified spider, a shy teenager gains spider-like abilities that he uses to fight injustice as a masked superhero and face a vengeful enemy.

programme.title: Venom
programme.desc: After a faulty interview with the Life Foundation ruins his career, former reporter Eddie Brock's life is in pieces. Six months later, he comes across the Life Foundation again, and he comes into contact with an alien symbiote and becomes Venom, a parasitic antihero.

programme.title: John Wick
programme.desc: John Wick is a former hitman grieving the loss of his true love. When his home is broken into, robbed, and his dog killed, he is forced to return to action to exact revenge.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 2
programme.desc: John Wick is forced out of retirement by a former associate looking to seize control of a shadowy international assassins’ guild. Bound by a blood oath to aid him, Wick travels to Rome and does battle against some of the world’s most dangerous killers.

programme.title: Tombstone
programme.desc: After success cleaning up Dodge City, Wyatt Earp moves to Tombstone, Arizona, and wishes to get rich in obscurity. He meets his brothers there, as well as his old friend Doc Holliday. A band of outlaws that call themselves The Cowboys are causing problems in the region with various acts of random violence, and inevitably come into confrontation with Holliday and the Earps, which leads to a shoot-out at the O.K. Corral.

programme.title: Tremors
programme.desc: A small town gradually becomes aware of a strange creature which picks off people one by one. But what is this creature, and where is it? At the same time, a seismologist is working in the area, she detects _tremors_. The creature lives underground, and can 'pop up' without warning. Trapped in their town, the town-folk have no escape.

programme.title: Tremors II: Aftershocks
programme.desc: Earl Bassett, now a washed-up ex-celebrity, is hired by a Mexican oil company to eradicate a Graboid epidemic that's killing more people each day. However, the humans aren't the only one with a new battle plan.

programme.title: Tremors 3: Back to Perfection
programme.desc: The now famous Burt Gummer returns to his hometown of Perfection, Nevada for the first time in years, but the deadliest Graboid evolution yet forces him to save the town he swore to protect.

programme.title: Werewolf of London
programme.desc: While on a botanical expedition in Tibet Dr. Wilfred Glendon is attacked in the dark by a strange animal. Returning to London, he finds himself turning nightly into a werewolf and terrorizing the city, with the only hope for curing his affliction a rare Asian flower, the Mariphasa.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: Seeking shelter from a storm, five travelers are in for a bizarre and terrifying night when they stumble upon the Femm family estate.

programme.title: Killer Klowns from Outer Space
programme.desc: When a small town is invaded by aliens from outer space who are capturing and killing the townspeople, no one takes them seriously. Why? The aliens all look like circus clowns, use weapons that look clown like, and all have painted on smiles. Only a few of the young people in the town realize the danger and of course no one believes them. Armed with an ice cream truck they try and rescue their friends.

programme.title: A Bucket of Blood
programme.desc: A dimwitted busboy at a beatnik cafe passes off a cat he accidentally killed and covered in plaster as a sculpture, prompting a demand for more art that compels him to commit murders.

programme.title: Bud Abbott Lou Costello Meet the Killer Boris Karloff
programme.desc: Lost Caverns Hotel bellhop Freddie Phillips is suspected of murder. Swami Talpur tries to hypnotize Freddie into confessing, but Freddie is too stupid for the plot to work. Inspector Wellman uses Freddie to get the killer (and it isn't the Swami).

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
programme.desc: Slim and Tubby are American cops in London to study police tactics. They wind up in jail and are bailed out by Dr. Jekyll. Jekyll has been murdering fellow doctors who laugh at his experiments. He has more murders in mind. At one point the serum that turns Jekyll into the murderous Hyde gets injected into Tubby.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
programme.desc: In Egypt Peter and Freddie find the archaeologist Dr. Zoomer murdered before they can return to America. A medallion leads them to a crypt where a revived mummy provides the terror.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: Killers from Space
programme.desc: Atomic scientist/pilot Doug Martin is missing after his plane crashes on an reconnaissance mission after a nuclear test. Miraculously appearing unhurt at the base later, he is given sodium amethol, but authorities are skeptical of his story that he was captured by aliens determined to conquer the Earth with giant monsters and insects. Martin vows to use existing technology to destroy them.

programme.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: After a terrified toy salesman is mysteriously attacked and brought to the hospital, clutching the year's most popular Halloween mask, Dr. Daniel Challis sets out to investigate the strange events and finds himself thrust into a nightmarish conspiracy.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: Bride of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Henry Frankenstein and his monster both turn out to be alive, not killed as previously believed. Frankenstein wants to get out of the evil-experiment business, but when mad scientist Dr. Pretorius kidnaps his wife, Frankenstein agrees to help him create a new creature, a woman, to be the companion of the monster.

programme.title: Creature from the Black Lagoon
programme.desc: A scientific expedition searching for fossils along the Amazon River discovers a prehistoric Gill-Man in the legendary Black Lagoon. The explorers capture the mysterious creature, but it breaks free. The Gill-Man returns to kidnap the lovely Kay, fiancée of one in the expedition, with whom he has fallen in love.

programme.title: The Rolling Stones Live From Rio
The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
G Love and Special Sauce - Woodstock 99 Act 03
Don Kirshner's Rock Concert - Steppenwolf-Ufo-Bad Company-Foghat Live (1974)
World Championship Wrestling - 3x09 - World Championship Wrestling 95
World Championship Wrestling - 3x10 - World Championship Wrestling 96
World Championship Wrestling - 3x11 - World Championship Wrestling 97
World Championship Wrestling - 3x12 - World Championship Wrestling 98
World Championship Wrestling - 3x13 - World Championship Wrestling 99
World Championship Wrestling - 3x14 - World Championship Wrestling 100
World Championship Wrestling - 3x15 - World Championship Wrestling 101
World Championship Wrestling - 3x16 - World Championship Wrestling 102
World Championship Wrestling - 3x18 - World Championship Wrestling 103
World Championship Wrestling - 3x19 - World Championship Wrestling 104
World Championship Wrestling - 3x20 - World Championship Wrestling 105
World Championship Wrestling - 3x21 - World Championship Wrestling 106
World Championship Wrestling - 3x22 - World Championship Wrestling 107
World Championship Wrestling - 3x23 - World Championship Wrestling 108
World Championship Wrestling - 3x24 - World Championship Wrestling 109
World Championship Wrestling - 3x25 - World Championship Wrestling 110
World Championship Wrestling - 3x26 - World Championship Wrestling 111
World Championship Wrestling - 3x27 - World Championship Wrestling 112
World Championship Wrestling - 3x28 - World Championship Wrestling 113
World Championship Wrestling - 3x29 - World Championship Wrestling 114
World Championship Wrestling - 3x30 - World Championship Wrestling 115
World Championship Wrestling - 3x31 - World Championship Wrestling 116
World Championship Wrestling - 3x32 - World Championship Wrestling 117
World Championship Wrestling - 3x33 - World Championship Wrestling 118
Columbo
programme.desc: Riley Greenleaf is a publisher and Alan Mallory, a successful writer, is his principal asset. But Alan Mallory has decided to change publishers. Riley threatens to kill Alan. Then he hires Eddie Kane, a Vietnam veteran, to murder Alan and to leave evidence that the killer is Riley himself, who has an excellent alibi. But a key will betray him.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Mrs Naugatuck has a party but does not invite Maude & Walter. When Maude accidentally breaks Mrs Naugatuck's urn, Mrs Naugatuck thinks it is revenge for not being invited to the party. Then Mrs Naugatuck purposely breaks an item of Maude's.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: While riding home on the subway one evening, Maude strikes up a friendship with a young West Indian woman. However, Maude discovers her wallet is missing after she leaves. Maude thinks the woman did it. The jokes on her when she discovers the woman is a new maid.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Rose has been dating Dr. Jonathan Newman and begins to think their relationship is leading to the altar. However she begins to have second thoughts especially since he's a midget.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy latest boyfriend Glen gives her a shock when he tells her that he's married. She surprises herself when she finds herself sneaking around to be with him. Meanwhile, Blanche unloads her old car on Rose in order to buy a new one.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: After Rose's boyfriend Al dies of a heart attack in her bed, Rose fears that she is a death jinx in the bedroom because her beloved husband Charlie died of a heart attack in her bed as well. To make matters worse, after Rose tracks down Al's sister to notify her of his death, she comes to find that his "sister" is really in fact his wife!

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: It is revealed that Blanche has another brother - a mentally handicapped man who has been living in an institution.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred is sure that his dreams will be good luck in picking numbers for a lottery and his hunch turns out to be true, despite the fact that Lamont forbids him to participate. Fred quickly finds himself $500 richer but also finds Aunt Ethel and Bubba needing quick cash.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: After getting in an accident with the truck, Fred thinks that he might have whiplash and Fred's friend Bubba convinces him that he can make big money off of the situation. Lamont is against the whole idea but Fred insists on going through with a lawsuit, but first Fred must identify the person the hit him.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike becomes the sole support of a growing family when Gloria loses her job because of her pregnancy.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie feels abandoned after Edith begins to volunteer part-time at the Sunshine Home for the Elderly.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie's company physical is fast approaching, and he needs to remain calm for an entire weekend for the blood pressure test. Of course, a variety of situations arise that test Archie's ability to keep an even temper.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Ben & Gary vs. Marlana & Raquel vs. Lori & Val
programme.desc: Lori & Val would later appear in the Sweep of Champions

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Laurie & Buddy vs. Teesha & Dea vs. Neef & Sooze

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges stencil all the wrong names on the office doors when they are janitors, which causes a rich old lady to think that Moe is really a famous decorator. She hires them to come redo her house, which ends disastrous, especially once Curly goes crazy from seeing tassels.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Set in the stone age, the stooges are cavemen who must have various misadventures hunting, gathering, and otherwise coping with prehistoric life. When some other cavemen threaten to take their women ("Aggie", "Maggie", and "Baggy"), the boys fight them off with a catapulting tree branch that shoots rocks and eggs.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Set in the old west, the stooges are three tramps wanted for vagrancy. After ruining a medicine peddlers show, they arrive in Peaceful Gulch where a picture has been printed declaring them to be three famous lawmen coming to clean up the town. Assigned to guard the bank, the boys have the local gang scared at first, but when the gang learns who the stooges really are, they rob the bank. The boys go in pursuit, find the bad guy's hideout, subdue the bandits and recover the money

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are actors who can't seem to find a job, so they decide to jump off a high building and end it all. On the roof top they meet three girl dancers with the same idea. Before they can jump, they meet a millionaire Broadway producer who hires them all for his next show. The rehearsal goes so well that he doubles their salary, but it all comes to naught when they discover that the "producer" is an escaped patient from Dr. Dippy's retreat.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Rejected by the armed services, the stooges decide to "do their bit" by becoming farmers. After paying $1000 and throwing in their car, the boys are owners of a run down farm, which lacks any livestock. After capturing an escaped ostrich, they decide to carve jack-o- lanterns for profit and then must contend with some Japanese who have escaped from a relocation center. The stooges become heroes by capturing the escapees, with the help of explosive eggs laid by the ostrich who had swallowed some blasting powder.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are hired as reporters and their first assignment is to get a picture of a visiting prince who is planning to marry a local socialite. The boys disguise as servants and infiltrate a party being in thrown in the honor of the prince. The stooges ruin the party, but save the day as they expose the prince as crook who is planning to rob the house. Their boss is so grateful for the expose that he gives the boys a bonus and the rich lady decides to marry Curly!

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Three bumbling waiters try to help a clinically depressed little girl by locating her kidnapped father. The Stooges operate a restaurant and try to help a little girl who becomes ill when her father, a bail bondsman, disappears with a lot of cash and the Stooges have to fight gangsters.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc: Sven brings us the 1945 Lon Chaney, Jr. feature, "Pillow of Death". Attorney Wayne Fletcher and his secretary are having an affair, so when Wayne's wife is found smothered to death, he becomes the prime suspect. As the police investigate the murder, a psychic with questionable motives tries to contact the deceased woman. Soon, Wayne begins seeing visions of his dead wife, and other people involved with the case begin to be killed, one by one.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne accompanies Jackie to her therapist after she chooses the ideal man for Jackie -- dull tax accountant Art; Darlene mouths off once too often about a female school bully.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne freaks out when she learns that Rodbell's employees must take a lie detector test to find out who has been stealing from the restaurant; Darlene bypasses Roseanne for advice about her beau.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: A pregnant Crystal snaps at everyone in sight, then goes into labor with Dan coaching her in the delivery room; Roseanne and Bonnie feud over who should replace Leon as the new manager of the coffee shop; and Darlene delivers a message to David to quit crowding her.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Mark turns to alcohol to solve his problems with Becky, and Dan lies to Roseanne about the boy's drunken, reckless evening at the Lobo Lounge to cover both their tracks.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The girls take Roseanne to the Beauty Box for Mother's Day in hopes of convincing her to let them attend a weekend rock concert.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: As the luncheonette closes and the bike shop folds, D.J.'s scholastic achievement is the only light on the Conners' dim horizon. (Coda: an ice-cream fight in the Conner kitchen; Arnie is abducted by aliens)

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne supports the family after Dan's motorcycle shop goes under; Becky considers moving to Minneapolis with Mark.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky and Mark return to say goodbye before moving away.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The department staff pose as Grace Brothers shareholders to avoid a takeover by a rival corporation. Meanwhile Captain Peacock finds himself working under obnoxious Mr. Lucas, who treats him in turn like a butler.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Poor Mr. Grainger. He's been behaving very curmudgeonly as of late, leading the staff to want him sacked or forcibly retired. Are they being too hasty, though? Can they still repair their friendship with him?

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Bill and Toony inform us about cartoon parodies.
Featured cartoons: One Meat Brawl (1947) I'm Just Wild About Jerry (1965) Alona on the Sarong Seas (1942) Pink Is a Many Splintered Thing (1968) A Squeak in the Deep (1966) Strife with Father (1950)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: A popular country western singer wants to get rid of his religiously zealous wife who's been using blackmail to control every aspect of his life. But a murder made to look like an airplane accident does not fool the wily Lt. Columbo.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline figure out a surprising fundraising solution when a potential storefront for Max's Homemade Cupcake becomes available for the girls to rent as the two go to great lengths to raise enough money to see their dream come true.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline decide to hire an intern to help take care of some of the menial tasks involved with the preparation for opening their new cupcake shop, but Max seems conflicted with her new managerial role as the boss

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: As the road to WrestleMania V continues, Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan present an exciting lineup of in-ring competition on this edition of Prime Time Wrestling, featuring a memorable singles bout between Bret "Hit Man" Hart and "The Million Dollar Man" Ted DiBiase. Plus, Jake "The Snake" Roberts, King Haku, and more in action.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: The Belchers beg Bob to buy a food truck to compete against the ones that are stealing his customers. When the "Lolla-Pa-Foods-A-Festival" comes up, the Belchers bring the truck and the kids decide to cause mischief. Unfortunately, this causes a riot to rise from the other food truckers.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra is upset when she sees Ray's giddy reaction to her friend's recently acquired "new and improved" breasts. Anxious to find out whether or not Ray is happy with her body, Debra decides to conduct a little experiment and makes some minor enhancement to her upper region.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: To fix his own dinner, Ray tries to open a tin of tuna with a new can opener Debra has bought. He spills tuna water and blames the can opener. This incident is shared by Debra and Ray with Robert and Marie respectively, but their viewpoints are radically different.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie recall the first time they met.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Arthur's 75th birthday is coming up and Doug and Carrie decide to throw him a surprise party, but they're in for a rude awakening when two of the invited guests are at each others throats and they can't find Arthur!

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine house-sits for Mr. Pitt. Jerry goes to meet up with her to go to the movies and winds up offending the Doorman. So to make up for it, Jerry watches the door. But when Jerry leaves his post and the couch in the lobby is stolen, Jerry and Elaine formulate a plan to cover their tracks. Meanwhile, Kramer invents a bra for men and George tries to find a way to get his father out of his apartment.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: A gym member teams with George in business and makes a date with Elaine to see Mel Torme; Jerry suspects his dentist of hanky-panky.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky volunteers to take over cooking duties for the Conners' Thanksgiving; Dan and Mark are still at each other's throats; Fred is shocked at the dysfunction of the family he might marry into; and Nana Mary reveals a long-kept secret about Bev's marriage.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Leon convinces Jackie and Nancy to open the restaurant for breakfast, causing Roseanne to fear he is planning to force her out. Roseanne's car is stolen -- by D.J., and her violent reaction frightens her that she may repeat the pattern of her own abuse as a child, while Dan blames her anger on work stress.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Pam is sporting a new look at work with her glasses. She admits she spent the night at a "friend's" house and forgot her contact solution. Michael makes sure to point out that the glasses make her look ugly, and if she wants to be hot, the glasses need to come off.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Jim is wearing his best golfing uniform because he is hitting the links with a potential client, Phil Maguire. This is a very important account for Jim because he needs to look good to Ryan, who recently put him on probation for his job performance. Andy and Kevin are also joining in the game to show their support.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Fleeing from a duel, Homer takes his family to live on a farm. There he discovers that by mixing tomatoes, tobacco and radioactivity from the nuclear plant, he can create a dangerously delicious new substance.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer briefly becomes famous after bowling a perfect 300 at the local ten-pin alley. However,his celebrity quickly wanes so he decides to bond with Maggie instead. He tries to teach her to swim but she ends up rescuing him after he gets into difficulties. As a reward he takes her bowling where she also gets the perfect score.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Due to overuse of fertility medication, Apu and wife Manjula give birth to octuplets. Desperate for money, Apu loans the babies to an unscrupulous carny promoter, then needs Homer's help to get them back.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After winning a classic motorcycle and becoming infatuated with biker culture, Homer decides to start his own motorcycle gang. The name Homer chooses for his gang, The Hell's Satans, is already in use by a different motorcycle gang and when they find out about it, they head to Springfield and kidnap Marge.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In this Christmas-themed episode, the students of Springfield Elementary are unwittingly used as a focus group to test new toys. The product of their labors, a cuddly doll named Funzo, is a lot more sinister than he appears.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al is haunted by a tune on the radio he can hum but not name, and he gets no help from Peggy or the D'Arcy's. Al similarly haunts Kelly's new boyfriend, Vinnie Verducci, the son of his fellow lowlife friend, Charlie Verducci, when Al warns Vinnie to only look and not touch, which is not easy for Vinnie.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al has to choose whether to spend his money on Bud's visit to the White House or letting Kelly enter a Miss Weenie Tot contest.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al and Peggy go over to watch a video at Marcy and Jefferson's house and proceed to take over the place. Meanwhile, Bud and Kelly seek gainful jobs for themselves, but only Bud gains with his first girlfriend, while Kelly babysits a family of seven kids from Hell.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: In a spinoff attempt of Married ... with Children, we follow the lives of Charlie and Vinnie Verducci. Charlie wants Vinnie to marry rich to get a better life and they go to a formal fund raiser to meet rich women.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al buys a cheap old air conditioner which leads to a massive blackout in the entire neighborhood, forcing the Bundy's to move into a supermarket.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: The Bundys and the D'Arcys face off to see who can spend the $1,000 shopping spree first. Meanwhile, Bud and Kelly bother celebrity Jerry Mathers who judges the contest.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: The Bundy car breaks down in New Mexico and they have to call Marcy and Jefferson for help.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: The Bundys and the D'Arcys face the effects of greed as they discover a gold mine in the desert.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: The Sanford home has become a campaign headquarters for the candidate Bob Williams and Fred wants nothing to do with it. However, once Fred discovers, Della Reese is working on the campaign of Williams' opponent, he goes all out for it even thought he's not for the candidate.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Donna receives a marriage proposal from another man and she decides to base her answer on whether or not Fred is ever going to ask her. Fred must make the choice now as to whether or not he is going to marry Donna. This leads Fred into turmoil and leads him to ask Elizabeth a heart felt question on whether or not she would object to him remarrying.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont is a judge for the Miss Watts Business Wife and the winner of the pageant wins $1,000. Fred quickly makes it clear that he will do anything to get his hands on the money. He even agrees to transform Aunt Esther into an elegant beauty as part of a bet he has with her husband, Woody.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont's friend, Ah Chew cooks dinner for the Sanfords one evening and a wary Fred is quite impressed at how good the food is. This leads Fred and Ah Chew to start a takeout business together with the headquarters in the Sanford home. Fred soon realizes that they should expand the business and turn the house into a Japanese restaurant.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris and Sharon show their true feelings when they become trapped in a meat locker together.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: After finding a dead rat in his carton of milk, Chris decides to become a food inspector.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Spot-gag about wildlife and nature.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Sniffles tries to stay awake and wait up to see Santa on Christmas Eve.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky shows a newsreel parody.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A dumb dog hunts a wisecracking quail.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A $10 bet is between Bugs and Cecil Turtle when they agree to compete in a race against each other. Bugs is sure that he'll win, because Cecil is a turtle, but Cecil has some tricks up his sleeves, unbeknownst to Bugs.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Grocery store products come to life, along with caricatures of Jack Benny, Rochester and Ned Sparks, and take-offs on Superman and King Kong.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A series of spot gags about farm life. Running gag: why are all the little piggies watching the clock so intently?

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A satirical night of Hollywood where a group of celebrities gather at Ciro's nightclub for dinner and dancing.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys want to sue a chick in class for sexual harassment because she's constantly giving them ""wood.""

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
The boys' class takes a field trip to an art museum.
videos:
Nirvana - "Heart Shaped Box"
Candyland - "Bitter Moon"
Matthew Sweet - "Superdeformed"

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Smart Beavis and Smart Butt-Head visit Earth and abduct Tom Anderson for human experiments; Beavis and Butt-Head are invited to sleep over at Cody's house when his parents mistakenly think they're good friends.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Butt-head rushes Beavis to the emergency room after an accident involving smoke, fire...and Beavis' butt.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head try to grow their own nachos as part of a school project.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Stewart has diarrhea; too much sugar turns Beavis into "The Great Cornholio."

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys get jobs as telemarketers; Beavis becomes the top salesman, and Butt-head can't figure out what to do.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butthead get called into work on a busy day. Butt-head gets out of it and sends Beavis in by himself, then goes in to harass him, and other customers as the restaurant comes to a standstill.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys photocopy a dollar bill and try to use the copies to buy stuff.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys incur the wrath of woman at the drive-in theatre, after taking a picture of her butt.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butthead kill another Friday night at the Maxi Mart. They try to service the girls by working the cash register and the manager, whose in the back room watching the camera, thinks that he's being robbed and calls the police.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: The fabulous Miss Yvonne just asked Cowboy Curtis out for a date -- and now he's as nervous as a prairie critter who's just been...um, asked out for a date by Miss Yvonne! To calm Curtis down, Cowntess the Cow suggests a little "date" role-playing -- and suddenly Curtis finds himself with "the most beautiful woman in Puppetland" -- Pee Wee? "Remember" the secret word and you'll have a "good" time!

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Randy steals Mrs. Steve's crummy crab apples from her tree, and Mrs. Steve needs Pee-wee's help in finding him. Then, Pee-wee plays the hands game with Tito, and "there" are enough apples for everyone at snack time.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline host a grand opening celebration at their new cupcake shop, and Max invites her former flames to show off her success.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: At therapy, Tony likens himself to the 'sad clown,' but Melfi doesn't buy into his melancholy portrait. Meanwhile, Carmela drags A.J. with her to Furio's house to offer decorating tips and companionship; Paulie puts the finishing touches on some free artwork; and Christopher finds himself at a crossroads.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: After a sitdown with Carmine and Johnny Sack, Tony contemplates the future of the HUD partnership. Meanwhile, Paulie blows off steam with an old pal; Janice starts losing patience with the obsessive Bobby; and Melfi ponders the Freudian undercurrents of Tony's latest dreams.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael approaches things from a new angle. Eleanor, Chidi, Tahani and Jason struggle to make a collective decision.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Tahani throws a dinner party to impress. Eleanor and Jason both have to lend a hand when things don't go as planned.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Chidi and Eleanor tackle a famous ethical dilemma, leading to a conflict with Michael. Meanwhile, Tahani harbors a secret and confides in Janet.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: With the Greens in King's Landing having seated Aegon II on the Iron Throne, Rhaenyra and Daemon count their dragons and prepare for war. Lucerys is sent on a diplomatic mission to Storm's End with Aemond seeking retribution as the course of Targaryen history is changed forever.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Jon Arryn, the Hand of the King, is dead. King Robert Baratheon plans to ask his oldest friend, Eddard Stark, to take Jon's place. Across the sea, Viserys Targaryen plans to wed his sister to a nomadic warlord in exchange for an army.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: While Bran recovers from his fall, Ned takes only his daughters to Kings Landing. Jon Snow goes with his uncle Benjen to The Wall. Tyrion joins them.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: House decides to awaken a comatose patient so he can question the man regarding the family history of his son, who may have a genetic condition and the father is the only living relative. Meanwhile, Wilson confronts House about the stolen prescription pad as Tritter approaches Cameron, Chase, and Foreman in an attempt to divide the team and reveal their loyalties.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: An 18-year-old teenager is brought to the hospital after having a heart attack. House reviews the boy's file and believes he has the diagnosis. He then turns the case into a game by sealing his opinion in an envelope and challenging Cameron, Foreman, and Chase to guess House's diagnosis on their own. Meanwhile, Tritter's actions against Wilson continue to strain the oncologist's relationship with House and destroy his ability to practice medicine.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart meets the original animator who created Itchy, penniless some 70 years after the studio stole his idea. When Bart helps him sue for restitution, he unwittingly bankrupts Itchy & Scratchy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Washed-up movie star Troy McClure is pulled over for driving without corrective lenses, and forced to go to the DMV to get his eyes checked. While there, he meets Selma, who he asks out for dinner. Spotted by a reporter, he quickly realizes that being seen in public with a woman will grab enough headlines to revitalize his career, leading to a whirlwind romance between him and Selma.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart creates a fake driver's license for himself and uses it to go on a spring break road trip with Milhouse, Nelson, and Martin. Meanwhile, Homer and Lisa bond after spending some quality time together at work.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and Milhouse wonder if anything interesting happens to the citizens of Springfield, which leads to a series of interconnected vignettes.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Raging Abe Simpson and His Grumbling Grandson in 'The Curse of the Flying Hellfish'
programme.desc: When a fellow veteran dies, Grandpa Simpson and Mr. Burns fight for buried WWII treasure as the only surviving members of their battalion.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Mayor Quimby uses immigrants as a political scapegoat, the Simpsons discover that Apu is an illegal immigrant.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: To show his kids how hip he is, Homer takes Bart and Lisa to a music festival, where he earns himself a place in the sideshow by catching a cannonball with his belly.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The end of the school year, and an empty yearbook causes Lisa to contemplate her lack of popularity. When Ned Flanders is summoned for jury duty and offers Homer a chance to stay at his beach house, Lisa sees this as the opportunity to reinvent herself as a slacker in the hope of gaining friends, which proves to be far more successful than she could possibly imagine --- but not if Bart has any say in the matter.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A Halloween trilogy. This time, Bart meets his "pure evil" Siamese twin; Lisa's science project attacks Bart; aliens Kang and Kodos invade as Clinton and Dole's replicas just before the 1996 Presidential election.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In an idyllic planned community, Homer works for a new boss who has trouble with the government. Meanwhile, the rest of the family has difficulty dealing with the bucolic burg.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: The Undertaker and "Stone Cold" Steve Austin defend the Tag Team Championship in a Four Corners Tag Team Match. The recent problems within DX come to a head. Val Venis reveals the results of his recent punishment.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira's estranged sister, Deedee, pays an unexpected visit, while David agrees to talk with one of Jocelyn's students about being "different".

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny tries to arrange a surprise birthday party for Moira. Things backfire when Moira, unwittingly, takes over the planning of her own party.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After Caroline has an incredible make-out session with Max's pastry school teacher, Chef Nicholas (recurring guest star GILLES MARINI), Caroline finds out something that makes her wish it never happened.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max finds herself wanting to be more than just friends with her pastry school partner, Deke. Meanwhile, Caroline is "hot for teacher" - the married Chef Nicholas.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Deke takes Max to his place after a great first date, his "home" is nothing like she expected. Meanwhile, Caroline feels empowered - then scared for her life - after having a shady car towed from the front of their apartment building.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Icing On The Cake
programme.desc: When Deke invites Max’s friends over for a gathering at his dumpster, Caroline learns an enormous secret about his background.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max decides to break up with Deke now that she knows about his wealthy background.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline asks Max and Deke to join her on her dinner date at Nicolas’s loft to help make sure she doesn’t do anything she might regret. Also, Max and Caroline treat themselves to very special hair appointments.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline goes into a tailspin and brings Max into the mix when Nicolas tells her he's going to leave his wife for her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline tries to fit in with Max and the diner gang as they go out to get wild and crazy at the St. Patrick's Day parade, but finds herself longing to practice her former holiday traditions at the Plaza Hotel.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Deke invites Max and Caroline to join him and his parents for dinner at their home, Max is tongue-tied until his mother confides in her about her secret past.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Wedding Cake Cake Cake
programme.desc: A girl who likes Max's cupcakes hires Max to make her wedding cake. Max who is still reeling from her break up, is hesitant to do it. She does it but the bride is constantly changing her mind about the cake and her marriage.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The New Lease On Life
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline's rent controlled apartment's lease runs out they track down the original lease holder to stay with them for a day to renew the lease. But events turn south when the old man won't leave until he gets some action.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline and Max join Sophie at the racetrack as guests of Sophie's new bookie boyfriend, Caroline's reignited love for horse racing gets out of hand.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline discovers that Max didn’t graduate high school by failing to show up for only one exam, she decides to help her best friend get her degree – and reconnect with her estranged mother.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: A reality show producer approaches Max and Caroline about shooting an episode of 'Keeping Up With The Kardashians' at their cupcake window.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max is embarrassed when she discovers her Tuesday night boyfriend is a DJ at a grocery chain.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: It's Christmas in Richmond. Rebecca enlists Ted for a secret mission, Roy and Keeley search for a miracle, and the Higginses open up their home.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Nate learns how to be assertive from Keeley and Rebecca. Ted asks Roy for a favor.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Ted is fired up that the new team dynamic seems to be working. But will they have a chance in the quarterfinal?

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Depicting Liza as the moon and Lester as the Sun, Beakman demonstrates the phases of the moon, answers meaningful questions such as "Do penguins have knees?", and explains how elevators work.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: It's microprocessor madness when Liza creates a video game to uncover the secrets of computers. Beakman dives into dentistry when Lester gets a cavity.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman's timid brother discovers that a visit to the doctor really isn't all that painful. Beakman also traces the evolution of oil from fossils.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Ben Franklin drops by the lab to help Beakman dispell some myths surrounding his inventions. Lester is shocked to learn that his recorded voice is different from the one he hears and Beakman reveals how chemicals react to their new partners.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Ants, Beakmania & Collisons--A day in the park is no picnic when a colony of ants make off with Beakman's food; Liza introduces Heimlich and his manuever; and Lester runs into trouble trying to explain collisions.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Bill Nye sheds light on solar flares, eclipses, sunspots, fusion, and solar energy, and visits an enormous solar energy farm outside Sacramento, California, to demonstrate how the sun is the source of energy for all living things on Earth.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Bill Nye's lecture is all about our brain which physically understands the words we see and hear. It controls our heart, our oxygen, our body temperature, and even our stomach. Our brain smells, sees, thinks, moves, and remembers all kinds of things all the time as long as it operates everyday.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin become unwitting accomplices in a stick-up at a gas station.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Principal Brown is concerned about a picture that Anais drew of her family. He decides the family is dysfunctional. Now Nicole has to stay home, Richard must get a job, and the boys work with Mr. Small to vent their energy.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Nicole is going shopping. Richard must work while she is gone but Gumball and Darwin show up to ask if he wants to play, but he doesn't want to. So afterwards they have a challenge to determine who is the laziest. Gumball and Darwin go to the convenience store to meet the shop owner Larry.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When a ghost named Carrie possesses Gumball's body in order to feel earthly experiences and to eat, he must learn to say "no" to her.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin's pranks on their father get out of hand.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin are ridiculed for their martial-arts outfits by their schoolmates, and their mom tries to convince them to take them off.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and his friends are invited to a high-school party hosted by Tobias's sister Rachel, but they must find dates in order to attend.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball decides to help Bobert act like a real boy. However, it soon becomes a battle for his very identity when the robot plots to take over his life.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball competes in a race in order to win Dad back.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg tells the other Titans that TV wrestling is fake and tries to teach them about real wrestling.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans are transported inside a fantasy adventure game and must find the key to return home.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Raven teaches the other Titans how to dance properly but then they are captured by the Dance Demon.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans reminisce about the time they threw a surprise party for Cinderblock.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans try to remember how to be superheroes in order to stop The Brain.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Santa tries to take over Halloween, it's up to the Titans and a few ghouls to stop him.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg and Starfire booby-trap the Tower for safety.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Starfire wins a goldfish at the carnival and vows to keep it alive.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Batman and Commissioner Gordon check in on various happenings around Jump City.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Santa decides to quit, the Titans pitch in to save Christmas.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: On Beast Boy's Birthday, Starfire complains because she has never celebrated a birthday on Earth. Beast Boy allows her to celebrate her birthday on the same day as his, but Beast Boy and Starlite get mad at each other after facing the struggle of sharing a birthday.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc:
Annoyed by Stimpy's stupidity, Ren goes to live off as a hermit, and goes crazy after months of being alone. He is kicked out of the hermits union for having imaginary friends.
Segment: Untamed World: Field Guide - Stimpy, reprising his role as a nature show host from Untamed World, in the jungles of Miami, Florida.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: The chums seek refuge in a model home of the future which is awash with rare conveniences.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy has a friend in his face (like a tick), then Ren ends up with the friend's friend, which starts to annoy him to no end.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren goes into Stimpy's body to find a medical explanation to why he is so stupid.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren & Stimpy go to the Natural History Museum to see the skeletal remains of the mammoth Stimpysaurus. Wilbur Cobb tells them about the evolution of the great beast and says that even the meat-eating Renosaurus couldn't conquer the huge bumbling idiot.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Famished, Ren & Stimpy pretend to be long lost children of two Farmers.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy are two starving peasants in a fairy-tale world, so Stimpy tries to wrangle some wheels of magic singing cheese from the village idiot by besting him in a contest of stupidity.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Renwaldo sends Stimpleton on a food mission to trade their last chigger for some cheese. Stimpy meets a village idiot and trades his chigger for the magical golden singing cheeses.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Mr. Anderson asks Beavis and Butt-head to go shopping for him at the drug store; they buy everything except what was on his list.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head finally manage to get their hands on some (non-alcoholic) beer and get "fit-shaced".

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head are recruited for an amateur game of football, the rules of which they don't understand.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After chugging Burger World's new, high-caffeine ""Volt Cola,"" Beavis transforms into Cornholio and is picked up by the Immigration Department and deported to Mexico as an illegal alien.

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Panthers (Irvin & Shaunda) vs. No Names (Colleen & John)

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Whatchamacallits (Mary Ellen & Fred) vs. Airheads (Nicole & Jon)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Green Slime is Scraped from Smokers Lungs will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast really begins to think that green slime is made from the mucus from the lungs of smokers.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Batman Gives Away His Batmobile will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
In order to get him slimed, Chris lets Patrick do the introduction. The make-up lady makes Stephanie look like a senior citizen. When the boom mike breaks, Ross makes Christian hang from the ceiling to operate the mike.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Rambo Takes Ballet Lessons will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross, in an effort to embarrass the cast, has their parents come to the studio.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Doogie Howser Gets Blamed for Sawing the Wrong Leg Off a Patient will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast is very upset that kids get blamed for everything.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Tony the Tiger Really Eats Choco-Bombs will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
All the show's secrets are uncovered. Ted finally finds the secret ingredient to the green slime.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Alf Learns to Cook Cats will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast tries to do an educational episode.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Kermit Treated Like a Prince will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross gets angry and revokes the cast's phone and ball-playing privileges.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
MacGyver Invents a Spaceship will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
To improve the cast and add a new edge to the show, Ross buys a robot to use as a cast member.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
You Can't Do That on Television
MXC
programme.sub-title: The Court Room vs. Rodeo Industry
programme.desc: It's gavel to gavel coverage in MXC land as the classic battle of busting broncos vs. busting criminals takes center stage. Games - Crossing the Pee-O-Grande, Wallbuggers, Little Man in the Boat, and Clear Sphere of Fear.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Hot off the success of the College Girls of Spring Break special, MXC holds their second ever all-women show, this time pitting the athletic sports types against the self-made business types. Meanwhile, Vic and Kenny pay tribute to Spike TV's new explosive show, Boom!, by playing with dynamite.
Games include: Great Holes of Glory; Rotating Surfboard of Death; Irritable Bowl Syndrome; The Giant G-String of Doom

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: White Collar Careers vs. Blue Collar Careers
programme.desc:
It's Career Day on MXC as white collar workers and their proteges compete against the blue collar workers and their aide de camps.
Games include: Plank Spankers; Slipped Disks of Doom; Mud Butlers; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Comic Book Industry vs. Personal Hygiene
programme.desc:
MXC presents the battle between the people who prevent stinkers against the drawers with pen-and-inkers when the hygiene industry challenges the comic book industry. Also, Vic turns Kenny's unsuccessful Oral-Facializer into the even more unsuccessful Man-Ipulator, and we see the adventures of Megaphone Man when he goes into the lair of the evil doctor Medula Oblongata.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Highballers; Gang Plankers; Runaway Stump

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
In honor of Spike TV's relationship with the the Ultimate Fighting Championship, this special edition of MXC pits the ultimate fighters against the Wisteria Lane gang and their compadres of Desperate Housewives in the battle of the octagoners and the Eva Longorias.
Games include: Take a Leek; Mudd Butlers; Sinkers and Floaters; Dash to Death; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Real Mafia vs. Video Game Industry
programme.desc:
MXC presents the goombahs and the game boys (and girls) in this battle.
Games include: Backyard Bocci Ball Bloodbath; Dope on a Rope; Nerve Racketeering; Slabs of Death; Sorry-Wrong Number; Clear Sphere of Fear

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Hollywood's Jilted Wives vs. Cheating Husbands
programme.desc:
The Brads attempt to get the upper hand on the Jennifers in an all-star edition of MXC to kickoff 2006.
Games include: Rotating Surfboard of Death; Pole Riders; Hand Job; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Big Bird takes on big bets when MXC brings together two of the most polar opposites in America: The kiddie playground of Sesame Street and the adult playground known as Las Vegas.
Games include: Cruelette; Wall Buggers; Big Bird Droppings; Sinkers and Floaters

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When Daria and Jane are assigned to make a student film, they decide to record a day in the life of Quinn.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria gets roped into participating in the Lawndale High yearbook and meets Ted, who up to now has been entirely schooled at home.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Jodie are invited to visit a school for the gifted and talented.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria develops a mysterious rash and has to check into the hospital for tests.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The school stages a medieval fair to raise money for a new library roof.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Whenever Jane isn't painting or creating other works of art, she like to run. So in order to spite the wrath of a tyrannical P.E. teacher, meet a cute boy, and keep herself and Daria from getting a failing grade in gym, she goes out for Lawndale High's Track & Field team and becomes an instant jock, much to the dismay of Daria, the quintessential anti-jock.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When Daria agrees to help Trent find a birthday present for Jane, they somehow end up at a piercing parlor offering a two-for-one special. Hmm.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria is assigned to write a short story turning people she knows into fictional characters. Like they're not unbelievable enough already.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold and Danger Mouse get transported to the year 1215 AD thanks to a fluke in the narrator's mic. They now have to act out a twisted version of Robin Hood starring Danger Mouse as Robin and Penfold as Little John.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Someone has stuffed the most important seats in England with cactus. Danger Mouse surmises the pointy plants come from the giant saguaros. Therefore, he and Penfold head for Mexico where they soon home in on Baron Greenback. Unfortunatelly, the villainous toad has an angry bull on his side.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Wufgang Bah, the world's worst composer, has used a ray to destroy all the music on the planet so people will have to listen to his. Dangermouse and Penfold are on the case, using a cassette deck with the last remaining tape of background music, without which they are powerless.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold's hearing problems make him immune to a device that brainwashes anyone who hears Greenback's voice.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A spaceship has crash landed in Birmingham city center. DM and Penfold meet an alien who is looking for a small furry creature called a Ticklehipos, that just loves to climb up trouser legs and multiply.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: The Spy Who Stayed in With a Cold
programme.desc: Agent 57 teams up with Danger Mouse to defeat Baron Greenback when Penfold stays home with a cold; the only problem is 57 gets it too and every time he sneezes he changes disguise.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Fireproof Handkerchief - Flashed Sign - Fishing Bears - Balancing Glass On Card - Lye & Aluminum - Water Hyacinth - Taste & Smell - Flight Simulator - Breaking Slat
